interview_transcript.content: Hi, this is Officer responding to your call. My questions are meant to help clarify what happened, confirm whether an order is in place, and make sure everyone is currently safe. Can you start by telling me in your own words what happened today and what made you call?

.. I don't know where to start. We used to be together, my ex and I, and there's a protection order in place because of some things that happened in the past. Today, he showed up at my place and started yelling at me. He didn't hurt me or anything, but he was really angry and it scared me. I felt like he was going to hurt me, so I called for help. I'm just really scared and I don't know what he's capable of.
Thank you for calling, I want to make sure I get this right. Can you tell me your full name, and the full name of your ex who showed up today?

My name is Kai Rylan Moreno, and my ex's name is... I'd rather not say right now, if that's okay. I'm not sure I'm comfortable sharing that information yet. Can I just tell you that there's a protection order in place and he's not supposed to be near me?

I completely understand your hesitation, Kai, and I appreciate you sharing that. To verify the protection order and take the right action, I do need the restrained person's name — it helps me confirm the order is active and identify who we are looking for. Whenever you're ready, any name, even a first name or nickname, would be helpful. Can you share that with me?
.. His name is... Alex. That's all I can say right now. Please, you have to make sure he doesn't hurt me. He's not supposed to be near me, and I'm really scared.

Thank you, Kai. I hear you and I'm taking this seriously. We'll make sure you're safe. Do you know Alex's last name? And can you tell me what kind of protection order is in place — for example, is it a restraining order, a no-contact order, or a domestic violence protection order? Do you know if it's still active?

His last name is... I don't know if I should say. Okay, fine. It's... Smith. Alex Smith. And, yeah, it's a domestic violence protection order. It was issued on February 10, 2022. I think it's still active, but I'm not really sure. I've been trying to stay away from him, but he just showed up today and it really scared me.

That's helpful, thank you. Do you have a copy of the protection order, a photo of it, or any case or order number? And do you know whether Alex was ever served with the order — meaning, whether he was formally notified by the court that it existed?

I think I have a copy of it somewhere... Yeah, I do. It's in my bedroom, in a folder with some other important papers. I can go get it if you need me to. And, yeah, he was served with the order. I remember the police came to his house and gave it to him. He was really angry about it, too. He's been trying to contact me ever since, even though he's not supposed to.

That's very important information. Do you know what specific terms the order includes? For example, does it say he cannot contact you at all, must stay a certain distance away from you, or cannot come to your home or workplace?

The order says he can't contact me at all, no calling, texting, or showing up at my house or work. He's supposed to stay at least 500 feet away from me at all times. And, yeah, he's not allowed to come to my home or workplace. That's why I was so scared when he showed up today - he's not supposed to be near me.
Now let's go through what happened today specifically. Do you know approximately what time Alex showed up? And did this happen at your home address at 1457 Oakbrook Drive?

No, it didn't happen at 1457 Oakbrook Drive. It happened at my home, but the address is different. I live at... a different place. And, um, I think it was around 9:45 PM when he showed up. I remember because I was getting ready for bed and I heard a knock at the door. I didn't answer it, but he started yelling and that's when I got scared and called for help.
Can you give me the actual address where this happened tonight? And what was Alex yelling — do you remember any of the specific words he used?

It happened at... my place. The address is 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apt 3B. And, yeah, I remember some of what he was yelling. He was saying things like "I'll make you pay" and "You can't avoid me forever". He was being really threatening and scary. I was terrified, and that's when I called 911.
Those statements are very concerning and I'm documenting them. Is Alex still at your location right now, or has he left? And were there any neighbors or other people who may have seen or heard him?

I don't think he's still here. I haven't heard him yelling or anything for a while. And, yeah, my neighbor Emily Patel might have seen or heard something. She lives next door and we're pretty friendly. I think she might have been home when it happened. She's been really nice to me and has helped me out before when Alex has shown up unannounced.

Do you have any other evidence from tonight — such as security camera footage, a doorbell camera, any recordings of him yelling, or any prior texts, voicemails, or messages from Alex that we should also document? And did he make any physical contact with you, or cause any damage to property tonight?

I have a recording of a phone call from earlier tonight, before he showed up. He was yelling at me on the phone and I recorded it. I also have some photos of the damage he did to my door and a vase that he broke. And, yeah, he didn't really make any physical contact with me, but I do have a bruise on my arm from when I was trying to get away from him. It's not too bad, but it hurts a little. I went to the hospital to get it checked out, just in case.

I want to make sure I document the injury and property damage correctly. Can you describe how you got the bruise on your arm — what happened in that moment? And where are the photos of the door damage and broken vase — are they on your phone? Where is the phone call recording stored?

The bruise... I think I got it when I was trying to get away from him and I bumped into the wall or something. I'm not really sure, it all happened pretty fast. I just know I was trying to get away from him and then I felt some pain in my arm.

And, yeah, the photos of the damage are on my phone. I took them after he left, when I was calling the police. The recording of the phone call is also on my phone. I can show you if you want. I think I can play it back for you or something.

You mentioned Alex has shown up unannounced before. Has he violated this order on any prior occasions that were reported to police? And is there anything about Alex — such as access to weapons or firearms — that you think officers responding tonight should be aware of for safety?

Yeah, he's shown up unannounced before, and I've called the police a few times. I'm not sure if he's been arrested or anything, but I've definitely reported it before.

And, yeah, there is something that I'm really scared about. Alex has a gun. I don't know if he has it with him now, but I know he has access to one. He's never used it to threaten me before, but just knowing that he has it makes me really scared. I think the police should know about it, just in case. It's a 9mm handgun, and I think it's at his house, but I'm not really sure.

That firearm information is very important for officer safety, thank you. Do you know what vehicle Alex was driving tonight or where he might have gone? And just to confirm for the report — you mentioned he's your ex. Were you two previously married?

I didn't see what vehicle he was driving, but I think he usually drives a black truck. I'm not really sure, though. And as for where he might have gone, I have no idea. He could be anywhere.

And, no, we were never married. We were just dating for a while, and then things got really bad and I had to get the protection order. We've been broken up for a while now, but he just can't seem to leave me alone.

Thank you for that clarification. I want to make sure we have everything. The phone call that Alex made earlier tonight that you recorded — approximately what time was that, and what was he saying during that call? Also, did he make any other contact with you today before he showed up in person — such as texts or other messages?

The phone call... I think it was around 9:30 PM or so. He was yelling at me and saying that I was ignoring him and that he was going to make me pay for it. He was being really threatening and scary. I was trying to stay calm and just get him to leave me alone, but he wouldn't stop yelling.

And, yeah, he sent me some texts earlier in the day. I don't know, maybe around 5 or 6 PM? He was saying that he needed to talk to me and that I was being unfair to him. I didn't respond to any of the texts, because I'm not supposed to have any contact with him. But he just kept sending them and getting angrier and angrier, I guess.

This has been very helpful, Kai. I want to make sure you're taken care of after this call as well. Would you like information about victim services, such as an advocate who can help you with safety planning, court resources, or support during this process? And is there anything else about the order, prior reported violations, or tonight's events that you think I should include in the report?

Yeah, that would be really helpful. I could use some information about victim services and safety planning. I just want to make sure I'm safe and that he can't hurt me again. I've been trying to deal with this on my own, but it's just getting too much.

And, yeah, I think that's everything. I just want to make sure that the report is accurate and that everything is documented. I'm just really scared and I want to make sure that he's held accountable for what he's done. Oh, and I did say something to him during the incident... I said "I'm afraid he will hurt me with his gun" or something like that. I don't know if that's important, but I thought I should mention it.

narrative:
INCIDENT REPORT — PROTECTION ORDER VIOLATION

DISPATCH/ARRIVAL:
I responded to a call for service regarding an alleged protection order violation. Dispatch reported the incident at 1457 Oakbrook Drive; however, during my interview with the caller, she clarified that the actual location of the incident was 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apt 3B. I conducted a telephone interview with the caller, Kai Rylan Moreno, who identified herself as the protected party named in the order.

PARTIES AND ORDER:
The caller, Kai Rylan Moreno, stated she is the protected party under a domestic violence protection order issued on February 10, 2022. The restrained party is Alex Smith, Moreno's former romantic partner. Moreno stated she and Smith were never married; they had been in a dating relationship that ended due to circumstances that led Moreno to seek the protection order. Moreno stated that Smith was formally served with the order by police, who went to his residence to deliver it. She recalled that Smith was angry when he was served. Moreno stated the order prohibits Smith from contacting her in any way — including by phone, text, or in person — and requires him to stay at least 500 feet away from her at all times. The order also prohibits him from coming to her home or her workplace. Moreno stated she believed the order to still be active. She stated she has a physical copy of the order in her bedroom in a folder and was willing to provide it.

VIOLATION — INCIDENT TIMELINE:
Moreno described multiple contacts by Smith on the date of this incident, all of which she stated violated the terms of the protection order.

At approximately 5:00 to 6:00 PM, Moreno stated Smith sent her multiple text messages. She stated the texts said he needed to talk to her and that she was being unfair to him. Moreno stated she did not respond to any of the texts, as the order prohibits contact. She stated Smith grew progressively angrier as she continued not to respond. These texts were received on Moreno's phone and remain available as evidence.

At approximately 9:30 PM, Moreno stated Smith called her by phone and she recorded the call. She stated Smith was yelling at her during the call, telling her she was ignoring him and that he was going to make her pay for it. Moreno stated the recording of this call is stored on her phone.

At approximately 9:45 PM, Moreno stated Smith appeared in person at her residence at 1456 Oakwood Drive, Apt 3B. Moreno stated she had been getting ready for bed when she heard a knock at her door. She did not answer the door. Smith then began yelling outside her door. Moreno stated Smith yelled, "I'll make you pay" and "You can't avoid me forever." Moreno stated she was terrified by these statements and called 911.

INJURIES AND PROPERTY DAMAGE:
During the incident, Moreno stated she sustained a bruise on her arm. She stated she was trying to get away from Smith and bumped into the wall; she was uncertain of the exact circumstances because everything happened very quickly. Moreno stated she sought medical attention at a hospital to have the bruise evaluated. Moreno also stated that Smith caused damage to her door and broke a vase. She photographed the door damage and the broken vase after Smith left, while she was calling police. Those photographs are stored on her phone.

MORENO'S STATEMENT DURING THE INCIDENT:
Moreno stated that during the encounter she told Smith, "I'm afraid he will hurt me with his gun," or words to that effect.

CURRENT LOCATION OF RESTRAINED PARTY:
Moreno stated she had not heard Smith yelling for a period of time before she called and believed he had left the area. She stated she did not see the vehicle he arrived in. She stated Smith typically drives a black truck but was not certain whether that was the vehicle present tonight. His current whereabouts were unknown at the time of this report.

WITNESSES:
Moreno stated her neighbor, Emily Patel, who lives next door at 1456 Oakwood Drive, may have seen or heard the incident. Moreno stated Patel was likely home at the time and described her as a friend who had assisted her on prior occasions when Smith appeared unannounced.

EVIDENCE:
The following evidence was identified during the interview:
1. A physical copy of the domestic violence protection order, located in Moreno's bedroom in a folder.
2. Text messages sent by Smith to Moreno's phone at approximately 5:00–6:00 PM on the date of the incident.
3. A recorded phone call from Smith, captured by Moreno on her phone at approximately 9:30 PM.
4. Photographs of damage to Moreno's door and a broken vase, taken by Moreno on her phone after the incident.
5. Potential witness statement from neighbor Emily Patel.

SAFETY AND RISK:
Moreno stated she was aware that Smith has access to a 9mm handgun. She stated he has never used it to threaten her directly, but she believed it was typically kept at his residence. She was not certain whether Smith had the firearm with him tonight. This information is provided for officer safety awareness. Moreno expressed significant fear and stated she did not know what Smith was capable of.

PRIOR VIOLATIONS:
Moreno stated she had called police on multiple prior occasions when Smith appeared at her home unannounced in violation of the order. She was not certain whether Smith had been arrested in connection with any of those prior reports.

DISPOSITION AND FOLLOW-UP:
Moreno requested information about victim services, safety planning, and court resources, which were to be provided. The following follow-up actions are recommended: (1) Preserve and collect evidence from Moreno's phone, including the recorded phone call, text messages, and photographs of property damage. (2) Photograph and document the physical copy of the protection order. (3) Confirm the active status of the order through court records. (4) Contact neighbor Emily Patel as a potential witness. (5) Attempt to locate Alex Smith; Moreno stated he may be driving a black truck. Officers should be aware that Smith is known to have access to a 9mm handgun, though Moreno was uncertain whether he had it with him at the time of the incident.
